question_text: Which of the following best describes the Law of Conservation of Energy?

options.label: A
options.text: Energy can be created and destroyed in any process

options.label: B
options.text: Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transferred or transformed from one form to another

options.label: C
options.text: Energy always increases in any system

options.label: D
options.text: Energy can only exist in the form of heat

explanation: The Law of Conservation of Energy states that energy cannot be created or destroyed; it can only be transferred between objects or transformed from one form to another. This is a fundamental principle in physics.
